Name: 0_colorado_state state_gov_republicans
Representative_Docs: ['WATCH: Gov. Polis calls out Republicans in State of the State (The Center Square) – Colorado Gov. Jared Polis delivered his last State of the State to a joint session of the Colorado General Assembly on Thursday. In his speech, the Democratic governor called out Republicans, reflected on his past seven years in office, and looked to policy priorities for his last 10 months in office. Since the Trump administration took office one year ago, the Democrat-led Colorado state government has often faced off against the federal government over policies and funding cuts. Polis reflected on that. “Over the past 12 months, it seems like Washington has often been in it to ... make life harder and less affordable, to make Americans feel more fearful, more belligerent and more vulnerable,” Polis said. “In the last month alone, the Trump administration has threatened over $1 billion in funding for Colorado.” As reported by The Center Square, Colorado has filed dozens of lawsuits to challenge federal Republican policies and cuts. Polis promised to keep up those efforts, while calling out Republicans. “We are fighting hard to protect every federal dollar owed to Colorado,” he said. “In partnership with Attorney General [Phil] Weiser, our state has successful', 'WATCH: Gov. Name: 4_passed away_grandmother_jane_memorial
Representative_Docs: ['Dora (Dori) Missirlian Crawford Birth: 1946 Death: 2025 A devoted and loving wife, mother, sister, grandmother, aunt, sister-in-law, mother-in-law, teacher, and friend to many. Dori touched every life she encountered. She was kind, humble, wise, independent, adventurous, fun, loyal, compassionate, giving, and spiritual…..a beautiful human being in every way. Dori passed away peacefully on November 1, 2025 in the loving company of her family. Dori was born on April 11, 1946 in Aleppo, Syria. Her parents, Haratune and Lydia Missirlian, moved with Dori and her older sister Sonia to Beirut, Lebanon before emigrating to Chicago. Eventually the family, which now included her younger brother John, settled in Fresno, where her father served for 32 years as minister of the Pilgrim Armenian Congregational Church. As a youth, Dori was an active participant in many church activities and loved singing and playing the piano. Dori graduated from Fresno High School in 1963 and Fresno State in 1967. After graduating, she began a career in education, teaching both in California and abroad. Her three years spent teaching in Europe sparked a deep love of travel, adventure, and exploration that stayed with her throughout her life. In', 'Jane Marie Solaro Birth: 1934 Death: 2026 Jane passed away peacefully on January 4, 2026 at the age of 91. Jane was born in Pacific Grove, CA on December 14, 1934 and lived in San Pedro her entire life. She is preceded in death by her loving husband John Solaro and her parents Matthew Bodul & Ann (Berkovich) Bodul. Jane enjoyed the last four years at the Canterbury in Rancho Palos Verdes with her loving friends and caretakers. She is survived by her loving children, Karen Solaro Durber (Martin), Denise Solaro and John Solaro (Lynn). She was a loving grandmother to Anthony Durber (Christina), Michelle Durber Dilly (Ian), Mark Durber, Nikki Solaro, Ali Solaro Sandoval (Dustin), Jack Solaro and Max Solaro and her great grandchildren Brooklynn and Logan Dilly She is also survived by her siblings Bob Bodul (Marylou), Sharon Bodul Werth (Fred) and Matthew Bodul (Angie) as well as several nieces and nephews. She will be deeply missed and remembered by all. Graveside burial at Green Hills Cemetery on Friday, January 23, 2026 @ 2 pm. Celebration of Life to follow. Honor with Flowers In memory of Jane Marie Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Jane Marie', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

Name: 5_arsenal_chelsea_injury_united
Representative_Docs: ['EPL results: Manchester United beats City in derby, Arsenal and Liverpool held to draw, Premier League scores, goals, news, video Michael Carrick’s Manchester United beat Manchester City 2-0 on Saturday (Sunday AEDT), dealing a huge blow to their neighbours’ Premier League title hopes, as sorry Tottenham slipped to defeat against relegation-threatened West Ham. Arsenal failed to capitalise on Man City’s loss, being held to a scoreless draw at Nottingham Forest. Liverpool were held to a 1-1 draw by lowly Burnley while Liam Rosenior celebrated his first league win in charge of Chelsea. Get all the latest football news, highlights and analysis delivered straight to your inbox with Fox Sports Sportmail. Sign up now!!! The early result at a bouncing Old Trafford — in Carrick’s first game at the start of his second spell in temporary charge of the Red Devils — left Pep Guardiola’s City seven points behind Arsenal. The Gunners could have extended their lead to a daunting nine points if they had beaten struggling Forest in the evening game, but were left frustrated with a 0-0 result at the City Ground. Former United midfielder Carrick was appointed as head coach until the end of the campaign following the sacking of Ruben Amorim earlier this month. The 44-year-old, who was also briefly United’s caretaker boss in 2021', "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Sport | Football Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Pressure was piling on Arne Slot after the Reds slumped to ninth in the table, but even without a number of key players, the champions were able to sweep Villa aside to move back up to third. They welcome Real Madrid - Trent Alexander-Arnold, Xabi Alonso, et al - to Anfield in midweek, before they take on Manchester City in a mouth-watering encounter at the weekend. But will they have any of their star names back from injury? Ahead of a big few weeks, here’s all the latest Liverpool injury news and return dates... Alexander Isak has endured a tough start to life on Merseyside. Subbed at half-time in Liverpool’s 5-1 win over Frankfurt in the Champions League, Slot later revealed the £125million summer signing had suffered a minor groin injury. “Alexander had to go off at half-time because he felt his groin a little bit. Name: 6_war_tehran_iran_president donald
Representative_Docs: ['US warns ‘the big one is coming’ amid conflicting signals over war’s duration London: US and Israeli forces have hammered Iran with new strikes against missile and drone launch sites, aiming to wipe out its capacity to hit regional military and civilian targets amid conflicting signals about the goals and duration of the war. The Israel Defence Forces launched simultaneous attacks against Tehran and Beirut on the fourth day of the conflict, targeting services in the Iranian capital and intensifying the war against Hezbollah in Lebanon, positioning troops at several points near the border. The IDF announced the latest strikes on Tuesday morning in the Middle East (4.30pm AEDT) after earlier confirming it had targeted the Iranian state broadcaster IRIB in Tehran and told civilians to leave the surrounding area. US President Donald Trump declared the war might go “far longer” than his previous estimate of “four to five weeks” and did not rule out sending ground forces into Iran. “I don’t have the yips with respect to boots on the ground,” he told The New York Post. Representative_Docs: ["Oscars Front-Runner Makes Shocking Confession About Her Husband's Cats: 'This Is Bad' Jessie Buckley won over critics and moviegoers with her heart-wrenching performance in “Hamnet,” but her comments in a three-month-old interview are receiving new scrutiny as awards season draws to a close. Appearing on the “Happy Sad Confused” podcast with her “Hamnet” co-star Paul Mescal in November, Buckley admitted she’d made her husband, Freddie Sorensen, get rid of his two cats early in their relationship. Buzz: Actor And Rapper Jailed For Killing Student In Hit-And-Run “Cats are mean,” she insisted. “My husband, when I started dating him, he had two cats ... this is bad ... One of the cats was, like, a pedigree model *****. And she staged, like, a coup against me. I’d come home, and there would just be, like, poo on my pillow.” Eventually, Buckley gave Sorensen an ultimatum ― which she joked may now get her “canceled.” “I was like: ‘It’s me or the cats,’” she recalled. “But I won! Sorry!” Mescal appeared to support his co-star’s actions, and quipped: “**** cats, honestly. **** ’em!” Advertisement Watch Jessie Buckley and Paul Mescal’s “Happy Sad Confused” interview below. Their comments on cats can be found around the 36:42 mark. Like this article? Keep independent journalis", 'Strictly Come Dancing fans have same complaint as La Voix withdraws from show \'Pointless!\' Strictly Come Dancing fans have been left with the same question after it was announced La Voix has withdrawn from the competition. During Saturday (November 22) night\'s show, the remaining contestants took to the dance floor at the iconic Blackpool Tower Ballroom. Before things kicked off for Blackpool Week, presenter Tess Daly informed viewers that the drag queen – real name Chris Dennis – would not be returning to the competition after sustaining a foot injury. She said: "We can confirm she has very sadly had to withdraw from the competition," before revealing there would be no elimination this weekend. But this has left fans wondering why Sunday night\'s show is still airing now that there is no dance-off. Taking to social media with their queries, one wrote: "So what’s the point of Sunday’s show when everything can be done tonight #Strictly?" You', 'Strictly Come Dancing star breaks silence after concern fifth contestant would quit Strictly Come Dancing\'s Alex Kingston is poised to carry on with her dance adventure, putting to rest concerns she might have to withdraw due to injury. The 62-year-old actress had triggered concerns about a potential exit after her daughter disclosed a rib injury on social media, stating that her mother had endured the discomfort to participate in Saturday\'s programme. The Doctor Who legend, who has been dazzling audiences on the dance floor alongside partner Johannes Radebe, was swift to comfort her supporters that injuries were simply "part and parcel" of the Strictly experience, and she had absolutely no plans to retire her dancing shoes. Fleur East, host of Strictly spin-off It Takes Two, has now verified that Alex will return to the competition following last week\'s Blackpool special. Name: 9_family_family members_gifts_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: Family, friends don’t seem to appreciate time I put into tedious Christmas cookie tradition Dear Eric: My late mother made a special Christmas cookie that required tedious decorating, considering each person’s personal preference for toppings, and giving them as gifts to family and friends. They always had a place of honor on our family Christmas table. When she was no longer able, she gave me the cookie cutter, and my daughters and I took over the family tradition. It takes the three of us several days to make, bake, decorate, package and mail cookies to family members around the country. We enjoy doing it and feel like we’re continuing the fun and festive tradition. However, in recent years, not all of the recipients acknowledge receipt. At some point in their lives, they have all helped Grandma decorate, so they know the time involved. Because of past delivery issues, I track the boxes and inquire if I haven’t heard from them in a couple of days. Last year, one response was “yes we got them.” This feels unappreciative! In the past most would text a picture of their children with the cookies, which we really enjoyed. We rarely see any of them anymore, as they are busy raising their families and don’t live nearby. This seems like a way for us to continue somewhat of a co', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Name: 11_laboratory_nurses_hospital_doctors
Representative_Docs: ['HNL Lab Medicine Brings Escape Room Fun to Hospital Education ALLENTOWN, Pa., Nov. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- HNL Lab Medicine is transforming hospital training by turning mandatory education into an adventure. To strengthen the collaboration between laboratory and nursing teams, HNL Lab Medicine\'s Clinical Hospital Advisors have introduced an innovative "escape room"–style educational game that turns essential lab‐related lessons into an interactive experience. The escape room begins with a familiar challenge: how to avoid getting a phone call from the lab about a rejected sample. From there, nurses progress through a series of puzzles based on real‐world scenarios, selecting properly filled blood culture bottles; answering type & screen questions; identifying best practices to prevent clotted or hemolyzed samples; ordering laboratory tubes correctly, and collecting laboratory tubes in the correct order of draw. Each correct answer provides a key or code to unlock the next stage, ultimately leading to a prize. The activity takes less than ten minutes per nurse, no longer than a standard training session, but delivers far more excitement and engagement. The format encourages problem‐solving, sparks conversation, and helps key lessons stick. Name: 13_tourism_world cup_olympics_events
Representative_Docs: ['Sporting And Cultural Events Boost Travel Intentions For 2026 Photo Credit: Deposit Photos Thirty-four percent of American travelers say they are likely to take an overnight trip to attend a sporting event in 2026. Twenty percent plan to attend a FIFA World Cup match, and 52% intend to travel overnight for cultural events such as concerts and festivals. Meanwhile, 47% are interested in touring Route 66 during its 100th anniversary year. These figures highlight a shift for the coming year where travel decisions are increasingly driven by events rather than relaxation. In 2026, Americans are choosing what to attend, not just where to go. The latest Longwoods International study shows that milestone celebrations and global competitions are now major motivators for travel. The calendar is driving demand. “Special events such as the FIFA World Cup, America 250 and the 100th anniversary of Route 66 will all motivate U.S. travel this year,” said Amir Eylon, President and CEO of Longwoods International. The industry has emphasized experience-based travel, but 2026 denotes a more specific trend. Events offer deadlines, shared participation, and reasons to plan trips in advance. The FIFA World Cup brings global visibility and international visitors. Representative_Docs: ["Dusk's £8 sale lamp that 'doesn't need cables' is 'so bright' without a plug The lamp has been reduced in price (Image: Dusk) A lamp can be the perfect finishing touch to a room - it can add a warm glow and illuminate any dark spaces while bringing style and colour. Luckily for shoppers, Dusk has reduced the price of one of its lamps, and shoppers say it is 'ideal for lighting up dark corners'. The Robin Reactive Glaze Ceramic Bulb Table Lamp in the colour 'Green/Brown' is currently available from Dusk for £8, down from £12. It also comes in the colour 'Cream' for £5, down from £12, and Dusk says it will 'add a touch of industrial chic' to any space. The Robin Reactive Glaze Ceramic Bulb Table Lamp's speckled glaze finish and exposed bulb create a raw, stylish look, Dusk says, and it is described as 'compact', measuring 9cm x 9cm x 15cm. The lamp is also battery-powered, making it perfect for bedside tables, desks, or any space that needs a little extra light. It is worth noting that three AAA batteries, which are not included, are needed to light the integrated LED bulb. However, there is no assembly required, meaning the lamp can be placed in the desired space and used straightaway. It comes in two colours at differing price points As an alternative buy,", "Dusk's white lace-trim spring duvet cover 'looks gorgeous on' and is reduced The Alice Duvet Cover can be easily styled with lots of different themes and hues(Image: Dusk) March signals the arrival of spring and, gradually, the days are growing longer and brighter. The Alice Duvet Cover comes in double, king and super king sizes and boasts a 200 thread count. It's worth noting that pillowcases aren't included and must be purchased separately, starting from £15 for one, for example. Representative_Docs: ['Buium, Demko lead Canucks to 2-1 win over Devils as post-Hughes era begins Published 12:10 pm Sunday, December 14, 2025 By Philip Wolf Vancouver Canucks defenceman Zeeb Buium, in NHL action Sunday, Dec. 25, 2025 against the New Jersey Devils at the Prudential Center in Newark, N.J. ( photo) Zeev Buium had a goal and an assist in his Vancouver Canucks debut, leading his new team to a 2-1 win over the host New Jersey Devils in NHL action Sunday afternoon at the Prudential Center in Newark, N.J. Buium, along with Marco Rossi and Liam Ohgren, were picked up by the Canucks in a blockbuster trade for Vancouver captain Quinn Hughes on Friday. Jake DeBrusk had the other goal for the Canucks (12-17-3), with Thatcher Demko sharp in making 25 saves for the goaltending win. Quinn’s brother Luke Hughes scored for the Devils (18-14-1). Former Canuck Jacob Markstrom stopped 13 of 15 shots and took the loss in goal. DeBrusk opened the scoring just 61 seconds into the game on the power play, knocking home his own rebound in front of Markstrom. It was DeBrusk’s first goal in 11 games and ninth of the season. Eight of those have come with the man advantage. Buium made it 2-0 at 6:48, when he flipped the puck toward the net and it banked in off a Devils defender. Name: 17_museum_egypt_egyptian_world largest
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 18_monitors_monitor_display_screen
Representative_Docs: ['Samsung OLED 48S95H: New 48-inch and 165 Hz OLED TV revealed ahead of schedule with HDR10 Plus Advanced support Samsung has already teased plenty of devices on the eve of CES 2026. However, the trade show has decided to lift the lid on one of Samsung\'s forthcoming OLED Smart TVs. For context, Samsung listed numerous unreleased Smart TVs on its Parts Database over a month ago. Of those, CES has announced that the S95H is a 2026 Honoree in its Gaming & eSports category. Specifically, the trade show has signalled out a 48-inch variant that was missing from November\'s leak. Incidentally, last year\'s S95F only went as small as 55-inches (curr. $1,997 on Amazon). According to the listing, the S95H offers a 165 Hz refresh rate with VRR technology supported. Assuming a 4K resolution from the S95F series and the usage of HDMI 2.1 ports, a 165 Hz refresh rate would require the usage of Display Stream Compression (DSC) to output at its peak resolution and refresh rate simultaneously. Moreover, the new 48-inch Smart TV will support Samsung\'s new HDR10 Plus Advanced format. Pricing and availability are unknown for now; the full description provided by Samsung and CES is as follows: The Samsung OLED (S95H) is the world\'s most advanced 48" OLED TV for high-end gaming. Combining unparalleled brightness and', 'Luckfox 16-inch 4K 120Hz portable touch monitor offers 100% sRGB coverage The segment of portable monitors continues to grow, with many models available on Amazon at relatively affordable prices, typically between $100 and $150. These screens can be useful for productivity on business trips or while traveling. But portable monitors that combine a high resolution with a fast refresh rate remain uncommon in the mid-range segment. Luckfox wants to address this gap with its new 16" 4K Touch Monitor. The 16-inch display features a 3840 × 2400 (WQUXGA) IPS panel with 350 nits brightness and a 1200:1 contrast ratio. It uses optical bonding technology with 6H-hardness toughened glass and a matte finish to minimize ambient reflections. The 16:10 aspect ratio provides more vertical workspace than the standard 16:9 format. It also supports a 120Hz refresh rate, which is an upgrade over the common 60Hz panels found in many portable monitors, such as the Auzai ME16Z01 or the Arzopa A1M; response times aren\'t disclosed, though. As for connectivity, the product integrates two USB Type-C ports and a Mini HDMI input. Both USB-C ports support audio, video, and power delivery, meaning that only a single USB-C cable is required when connected to a compatible smartphone, tab', "World's largest monitor company designed a 4K 48-inch OLED dual-sided smart gaming display with metal feet, a magnetic power socket, and an Alienware-inspired sound system - but who will actually use it? Sign up for breaking news, reviews, opinion, top tech deals, and more. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful GP TV has unveiled what it calls the world’s first dual-sided 48-inch 4K OLED television, designed for gaming and sports viewing. The display is intended to deliver immersive experiences on both sides simultaneously, with built-in audio and dynamic lighting effects. TPV Technology Group, the company behind GP TV, claims the screen targets premium users who value high-end visual fidelity, as well as the public sector and government institutions. This design feels unusual because most monitors or televisions sit against a wall or on a desk, making a rear-facing display largely unnecessary. Its relevance only emerges if the screen stands in the center of a room, similar to display setups commonly seen in shopping malls - and even then, deploying such a device in public spaces would increase the risk of physical damage, leaving the practical use case for this product uncertain. It comes with an audio system that integrates surround sound, Dolby Atmos support, and sound tracking that follows on-screen action. Speakers with fabric covers on both sides creat"]

Name: 20_welfare_wages_labour_rising costs
Representative_Docs: ['How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Benefits Budget 2025 Child Benefit Department for Work and Pensions DWP Pensions PIP Rachel Reeves Universal Credit National government Personal Finance DWP benefits including Universal Credit, Child Benefit, PIP, Carer\'s Allowance, and the State Pension are to increase in April 2026 after Rachel Reeves announced higher payments in her budget speech today. Anyone who claims DWP benefits should be in line for an increase in the next financial year, as the chancellor laid out plans to help pensioners, disabled people, parents, low earners, carers and unemployed people keep up with rising costs. Addressing MPs, Ms Reeves said: "The broken welfare system that we inherited wrote off millions of people as too sick to work and we will reform that system so that it is a system that does not count the cost of failure, but one that protects people who cannot work and empowers those who can. "We have brought back face-to-face assessments for disability benefits. Those are the face-to-face assessments that the shadow chancellor (Sir Mel Stride) got rid of when he was work and pensions secretary and our changes we have made to Universal Credit will get 15,000 people back into work, a figure con', 'How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Name: 23_immigration_britain_british government_immigrants
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Name: 24_federal reserve_florence_meeting_economy
Representative_Docs: ['Florence Press Conference Confirms ECB Remains Comfortable in Its Good Place Even a change of scenery has done nothing to change the European Central Bank’s current wait-and-see stance. Florence, where the ECB met today for its only annual meeting outside of Frankfurt, is apparently also this ’good place’, the central bank’s well-known pseudonym for comfortably keeping interest rates on hold. ECB President Christine Lagarde even called it a ‘magnificent’ place – Florence, that is, not rates. The comments at the press conference confirmed once again that the ECB has no intention to leave its ‘good place’ any time soon. We had feared a rather uneventful press conference. What should we say? We hate to be right… Anyhow, here are a few interesting remarks made by Lagarde: On the ECB’s risk assessment, the introductory statement had the most remarkable change compared with September. Last month, the ECB had still talked about risks to the growth outlook that had become “more balanced”; today, it refrained from qualifying any balance of risk. Instead, the central bank had a somewhat more extensive list of upside and downside risks to the growth outlook. Lagarde even said that some of the downside risks had abated. Name: 25_village_scenic_accommodation_wales
Representative_Docs: ["The pretty little seaside village surrounded by incredible winter walks A gorgeous seaside village with scenic winter walks is the perfect place to spend a day exploring and enjoying a winter stroll. Charmouth, along the border of Dorset and Devon, boasts an abundance of footpaths and unspoilt countryside land with panoramic views of the English Channel. Harbouring a unique charm of seaside air, the village is an idyllic sanctuary for walkers who want a gentle pace and those hoping for a more uphill challenge. The South West Coast Path is the longest path in Britain, stretching all the way from Minehead in Somerset to Poole Harbour in Dorset. The journey can start in Charmouth, with an 83 mile walk across the astonishing coastline. Walkers can enjoy spectacular views of the far reaching coast and landmarks such as Durdle Door. It's a lengthy journey with a challenging route, so it’s recommended for more experienced walkers. Read more: The stunning winter walk that has been named the 'most scenic' in the UK Read more: The pretty European city that’s one of Europe’s most walkable – best in winter We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties bas", "'Slice of heaven' holiday let with breathtaking views and a hot tub is a 'hidden gem' Visitors love the views from the Dreamcatcher Wagon(Image: Holiday Cottages) We might have found the ideal glamping break solution for those looking to get away from it all and be surrounded by nature. The Dreamcatcher Wagon is nestled in the village of Craswall, close to the border with Wales and enveloped by the dramatic landscape of the Black Mountains. The setting provides an ideal mix of tranquillity and chances to explore. Village shops with local produce and a traditional pub are within easy walking distance. Close to Hay‐on‐Wye, on the fringes of the Brecon Beacons National Park, the accommodation boasts stunning vistas across the surrounding countryside, allowing guests to feel truly connected with nature. It also has a private hot tub among its creature comforts. March availability for a seven-night break is currently priced at £488, down from £537 and there is still some availability through spring and summer. Perfect for two guests, the secluded off-grid retreat sits within a tranquil 17-acre farm that's home to horses, donkeys and chickens. The wagon boasts an attractive arched roof and carefully crafted interiors, delivering a warm yet elegant ambience. Name: 26_renovations_homes_construction_downtown
Representative_Docs: ['Builder buys 53 townhouse sites in St. Pete waterfront development for $12M Loading Mattamy Homes has bought 53 townhouse sites in St. Petersburg’s Amara Bay Residences & Marina master-planned community currently under construction. The sites at 12000 Gandy Blvd. sold for $12.64 million. Collier, which brokered the deal and announced the sale, says in a statement that the lots are fully entitled and approved as part of the master-planned community. Construction of the townhouses is expected to begin in coming weeks with sales starting in the summer. Mattamy Homes, Collier says in a statement, will develop two townhome designs with two stories of living space. They will range from 2,371 square feet to 2,470 square feet with three to four bedrooms, two-and-a-half baths and two-car garages. Pricing is expected to start in the $700,000s. Amara Bay is a 39-acre mixed-use development just as you come into Pinellas County from the Gandy Bridge. It is being developed by the Miami firm Key Development, which sold the lots to Mattamy Homes. Plans for the master-planned community call for 415 luxury apartments, a 150-slip marina, an 8,000-square-foot restaurant and 3,000 feet of walkable waterfront. The new Revive showroom will be at 601 S. New York Ave. in Winter Park. The 5,000-square-foot shop is currently under construction and expected to open this spring. (The property is the former home of Ethos Vegan Kitchen, a restaurant that closed in 2024.) Justin Caballero, Revive’s founder and president, says in a statement that the company is investing in the Orlando market because it “sees the same dynamics that fueled our success in Tampa, rapid growth, strong home values, and a high concentration of homes ready for thoughtful, award-winning, high-end renovations.” The company says it expects to profit from a strong demand for remodels in Orlando’s Windemere neighborhood and Winter Park. Revive Design and Renovation’s Tampa showroom and design center is at 3102 W. Toll Brothers at Elyson offers an exceptional selection of one- and two-story single-family homes across three distinct collections. Front row (L-R): Betty Anne Marino, Susan Clovechok, Ron Oszust, Suzan Hewat, Melanie Jeannotte, Christine Hoechsmann. Back row (L-R): Am Naqvi, Jared Basil, Owen Torgerson, Katrine Conroy. Missing: Angus Graeme and Karen Hamling (Submitted) Four new directors have been appointed to the Columbia Basin Trust’s Board of Directors. Melanie Jeannotte (Fernie), Jared Basil (yaqan nuʔkiy), Am Naqvi (Nelson), and Katrine Conroy (Pass Creek) are joining the 12-person team, bringing their own diverse business and leadership expertise to the table. Name: 28_oil gas_state lawmakers_california_drilling
Representation: ['oil gas', 'state lawmakers', 'california', 'drilling', 'santa barbara', 'fossil fuels', 'clean energy', 'coastal', 'renewable energy', 'pacific coast']
Representative_Docs: ["California lawmakers push back against offshore oil drilling (The Center Square) – Weeks after California Lt. Gov. Eleni Kounalakis spoke out against federal efforts to expand offshore oil drilling, state lawmakers told The Center Square that increased drilling is deeply unpopular among coastal residents. Opponents warn against the environmental costs. But supporters say technology has made drilling safer. They also note offshore drilling could boost America's energy independence and lower gas prices in California, which typically has the highest in the U.S. Legislators’ comments opposing the drilling come after the announcement in November 2025 that the U.S. Department of the Interior would expand oil and gas drilling leases not just off the Pacific Coast in areas such as Santa Barbara, but other sites on the nation’s outer continental shelf. “We have a deep, visceral experience that is seared into the community’s consciousness about the risks of offshore oil development,” Assemblymember Gregg Hart, D-Santa Barbara, told The Center Square. “We are adamantly opposed to the leasing. There’s been a bipartisan consensus for 40 years that we want to wind down offshore oil development, not expand it.” According to a November 2025 order from the U", "California lawmakers push back against offshore oil drilling (The Center Square) – Weeks after California Lt. Name: 29_surgical_hospital_memorial hospital_patients
Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques.
